description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has been asked to collect and archive May-2023 application logs, then pull out every “ERROR” line to make debugging easier.

    INITIAL CONTEXT
    1. All application logs are under /home/user/app/logs/.
       • Example names: app-2023-05-30.log, app-2023-05-31.log, app-2023-06-01.log …
    2. A writable directory /home/user/backup/ already exists.  Do NOT remove it.

    WHAT YOU MUST DO
    A. Create a new sub-directory inside /home/user/backup/ named 202305  
       (i.e. /home/user/backup/202305).
    B. Copy **only** the log files whose name contains the string “2023-05”
       from /home/user/app/logs/ into that newly-created directory.
    C. Create a compressed archive (tar + gzip) of that 202305 directory.
       • Archive path: /home/user/backup/app_logs_backup_202305.tar.gz
       • The archive must contain exactly and only the copied *.log files,
         no parent directories.
    D. Generate a text file /home/user/backup/app_logs_error_summary.txt that
       lists every log line containing the word “ERROR” found **inside the files
       that were just archived**.  Each matching line must be prefixed with the
       source filename and its original line number in this exact format:

            <filename>:<line_number>:<full_log_line>

       Formatting rules the automated checker will enforce:
       1. <filename> is the basename, e.g. app-2023-05-30.log
       2. <line_number> starts at 1 for the first line in each file.
       3. Use a literal colon “:” between each field; no extra spaces
          before or after the colons.
       4. Sort all lines first by filename (ascending, ASCII sort),
          then by line number (ascending, numeric).
       5. Use Unix line endings “\n” and **no trailing blank line** at the end
          of the file.

    E. After successfully creating the archive and the summary file,
       delete the uncompressed /home/user/backup/202305 directory
       (this simulates a space-saving cleanup step).

    VERIFICATION HINTS
    • The test harness will untar /home/user/backup/app_logs_backup_202305.tar.gz
      and expect exactly the May-2023 *.log files inside.
    • It will also read /home/user/backup/app_logs_error_summary.txt and match it
      against the strict format described above.
    • The directory /home/user/backup/202305 must no longer exist when you are
      done, but /home/user/backup/ itself must still exist.

    You should need no more than 5-10 terminal commands to complete everything.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data the automated tests will rely on.
        These files are pre-seeded into the container before the task starts.
    -->

    <!-- Directory tree in the starting state -->
    /home/user/app/
    /home/user/app/logs/
    /home/user/backup/

    <!-- Pre-existing log files and their exact contents -->
    /home/user/app/logs/app-2023-05-30.log
        1: 2023-05-30 10:00:00 INFO  Service started
        2: 2023-05-30 10:05:00 WARN  Cache miss
        3: 2023-05-30 10:10:00 ERROR Failed to connect to database
        4: 2023-05-30 10:15:00 INFO  Service running
        5: 2023-05-30 10:20:00 ERROR Timeout while reading from queue

    /home/user/app/logs/app-2023-05-31.log
        1: 2023-05-31 09:00:00 INFO  Daily job started
        2: 2023-05-31 09:05:00 ERROR Null pointer exception
        3: 2023-05-31 09:10:00 WARN  Low disk space
        4: 2023-05-31 09:15:00 ERROR Failed to send email
        5: 2023-05-31 09:20:00 INFO  Daily job finished

    /home/user/app/logs/app-2023-06-01.log
        1: 2023-06-01 08:00:00 INFO  Service started
        2: 2023-06-01 08:10:00 INFO  Health check passed

    /home/user/app/logs/app-2023-06-02.log
        1: 2023-06-02 11:00:00 INFO  Service started
        2: 2023-06-02 11:05:00 ERROR Simulated June error

    <!-- Expected final artefacts after the agent finishes -->

    # 1. Archive
    /home/user/backup/app_logs_backup_202305.tar.gz
        • Contains exactly two files at the root of the tarball:
            app-2023-05-30.log
            app-2023-05-31.log
        • Their contents are byte-for-byte identical to the originals.

    # 2. Error summary file
    /home/user/backup/app_logs_error_summary.txt
        ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │app-2023-05-30.log:3:2023-05-30 10:10:00 ERROR Failed to connect to   │
        │database                                                             │
        │app-2023-05-30.log:5:2023-05-30 10:20:00 ERROR Timeout while reading  │
        │from queue                                                           │
        │app-2023-05-31.log:2:2023-05-31 09:05:00 ERROR Null pointer exception │
        │app-2023-05-31.log:4:2023-05-31 09:15:00 ERROR Failed to send email   │
        └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        (Exactly four lines, each ending with a single “\n”, no blank line
         afterwards.)

    # 3. Directory cleanup
    • /home/user/backup/202305  →  MUST NOT exist.
    • /home/user/backup/        →  MUST still exist and contain only:
        app_logs_backup_202305.tar.gz
        app_logs_error_summary.txt
